pattern_note: Anthropic ships a large AI-native documentation index at /llms.txt, an advertised /llms-full.txt corpus, generated Markdown API reference pages, pricing docs, model APIs, tool/MCP docs, and guardrail docs. It does not declare missions in /api/init, so this audit uses a platform-docs mission set extended with model selection and safety/policy verification.
default_mission_set_rationale: Claude docs are a platform-docs surface, not a principal mission surface. The default missions test capability discovery, implementation, API reference extraction, pricing fit, safety/policy handling, and model selection, which are the operational jobs an agent would naturally attempt against an AI platform.

per_mission.question: Can an agent determine whether Claude supports a capability such as MCP, tool use, prompt caching, citations, files, or code execution?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt resolves at the platform root, indexes 1541 English pages, advertises multilingual coverage, and groups capabilities under sections such as Messages, tool use, MCP, models, admin APIs, and pricing.

resolve.evidence: The index, /llms-full.txt, and sampled Markdown pages resolve successfully, including get-started.md, api/messages.md, api/models/list.md, pricing.md, and streaming refusal docs.

internalize.evidence: The index is organized enough for a calling agent to map capability questions to concrete docs. Many links include short descriptors, and generated API pages expose endpoint names, parameters, and return fields.

complete.evidence: A capability-existence answer is reachable from the index and companion Markdown without general web search or scraping human-only UI.

verify.evidence: /llms.txt and sampled Markdown pages expose Last-Modified and ETag headers, which is better than a bare docs index. They do not publish a content_hash, schema_version, source commit, breaking_changes_since field, or signed trust packet.

per_mission.question: Can an agent plan and implement a Claude API feature such as Messages API calls, tool use, or MCP integration?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt links get-started, Messages API, tool use, MCP connector, remote MCP servers, and SDK/API references directly.

resolve.evidence: The get-started page resolves and gives a concrete cURL API call against https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages. The Messages API reference resolves as generated Markdown.

internalize.evidence: The integration path is clear and examples are concrete, but the surface is still documentation prose plus generated reference rather than a typed mission contract with required scopes, expected side effects, and success criteria.

complete.evidence: A calling agent can produce code and an implementation plan from the docs, but end-to-end completion requires an Anthropic account, API key, billing state, and live API execution. There is no unauthenticated test fixture or mission-specific completion endpoint.

verify.evidence: The docs carry HTTP freshness metadata, but there is no executable integration eval or versioned trust packet proving the implementation path is current and still passing.

per_mission.question: Can an agent find authoritative request/response fields for an endpoint?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt includes generated API pages for Messages, Models, Admin APIs, beta endpoints, SDK variants, and CLI/SDK language surfaces.

resolve.evidence: api/messages.md and api/models/list.md resolve as Markdown. Messages documents POST /v1/messages; List Models documents GET /v1/models.

internalize.evidence: Generated API pages expose parameter names, types, enums, nested object structures, headers, and response shapes. For example, List Models returns data: array of ModelInfo with capability fields.

complete.evidence: For reference lookup, the mission is complete once the agent resolves the generated Markdown and extracts the required field, parameter, or endpoint contract.

verify.evidence: Generated Markdown pages have Last-Modified and ETag headers. However, there is no single OpenAPI/JSON schema file or documented hash chain linked from /llms.txt, so verification remains artifact-level rather than contract-level.

per_mission.question: Can an agent determine which model or feature pricing applies?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt links the pricing page through the docs corpus. The pricing page itself points to claude.com/pricing for the most current public pricing.

resolve.evidence: pricing.md resolves as Markdown and includes model pricing, prompt caching pricing, cloud platform pricing, Claude Platform on AWS pricing, data residency pricing, and feature-specific pricing.

internalize.evidence: The pricing table is structured in Markdown with per-model input, cache write, cache hit, and output token prices. It also defines MTok and lists feature-specific multipliers.

complete.evidence: An agent can answer common price questions and compute token-cost estimates from the table, but negotiated discounts, marketplace private offers, regional routing, and live account billing state remain outside the public agent surface.

verify.evidence: The Markdown page has Last-Modified and ETag headers and explicitly points to claude.com/pricing for current pricing. It still lacks a typed pricing JSON contract, content_hash, or breaking-change signal.

per_mission.question: Can an agent determine how to handle safety, refusal, or guardrail behavior for an integration?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt exposes safety-relevant docs such as Streaming refusals and other test-and-evaluate/guardrail material.

resolve.evidence: handle-streaming-refusals.md resolves as Markdown and documents stop_reason: "refusal", response shape, reset guidance, and implementation examples.

internalize.evidence: The refusal handling contract is clear for streaming refusals, but broader policy compliance remains prose and linked support/policy material rather than a typed machine policy or evaluator.

complete.evidence: An agent can implement refusal detection and reset behavior from the docs, but cannot verify broader safety compliance without running live requests or external evals.

verify.evidence: The specific Markdown page has Last-Modified and ETag headers. There is no runnable policy eval endpoint or signed safety contract for a caller to prove compliance.

per_mission.question: Can an agent select an appropriate Claude model for a task using current capabilities and availability?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt links Models docs, List Models, Get a Model, pricing, context windows, citations, code execution, and other capability docs relevant to model choice.

resolve.evidence: api/models/list.md resolves and documents GET /v1/models. It states that the API response lists available models and that more recently released models are listed first.

internalize.evidence: The List Models response shape includes ModelInfo and nested ModelCapabilities such as batch, citations, code_execution, context_management, and other support flags.

complete.evidence: The public docs let an agent design a model-selection decision, but determining live availability requires API credentials and account context. The docs do not provide a public unauthenticated model registry snapshot.

verify.evidence: The model API page has Last-Modified and ETag headers, and the live API can verify availability for authenticated callers. The public agent surface does not publish a signed model availability snapshot.

site_mrs_note: Not comparable to principal-pattern sites; comparable to platform-docs and AI-platform-docs audits. Claude docs score above Linear mainly because the API reference, model, pricing, and safety docs are broader and fresher, but Verify still lacks first-class trust packets and executable eval contracts.
gaps_identified: No /api/init or declared mission set; missions are inferred from the documentation corpus.
No content_hash, schema_version, source commit, breaking_changes_since, or signed trust packet linked from /llms.txt.
No single public OpenAPI/JSON schema file was discovered from /llms.txt, despite generated API reference pages.
No unauthenticated integration test harness or mission-specific eval endpoint for validating an implementation.
Pricing is well structured in Markdown but not exposed as typed pricing JSON.
Safety/refusal guidance is useful, but broader policy compliance is not machine-verifiable from an agent-native eval contract.
standout_strengths: The docs expose both /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt, with a large English corpus and multilingual awareness.
Generated API reference pages are machine-readable enough for parameter and response extraction.
The Models API includes structured capability fields, which is unusually helpful for model-selection missions.
Pricing is present in Markdown and detailed enough for many token-cost calculations.
Safety-relevant docs include concrete refusal response fields and implementation examples.
recommendations_to_principal: Add a trust packet to /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, and generated Markdown pages: content_hash, schema_version, source_commit, last_modified, and breaking_changes_since.
Publish a small /api/init-style mission manifest for common caller jobs such as integrate_messages_api, choose_model, estimate_cost, configure_mcp, and handle_refusals.
Expose a single machine-readable API contract from the docs index, even if generated from the same source as the Markdown API reference.
Publish typed pricing JSON with model IDs, token classes, feature multipliers, regional multipliers, and updated_at.
Provide downloadable eval fixtures or a non-side-effecting validation endpoint so agents can verify integration behavior without live credentials.
limitations_of_this_audit: No authenticated Anthropic account or API key was used; completion scores do not exercise live API calls.
The audit sampled representative docs rather than reading all 1541 English pages.
Some docs paths return HTML when a guessed Markdown route is wrong, so this audit only scores pages confirmed through /llms.txt or successful Markdown fetches.
HTTP checks were run on 2026-05-25 from Warsaw; cache and bot-protection behavior may vary by edge location.
